concepts.title: Mandal Commission
concepts.description: The Mandal Commission was established in 1979 to identify the socially and economically backward classes in India. It recommended 27% reservation for OBCs in government jobs, which was implemented in 1990.

concepts.title: Judicial Activism
concepts.description: Judicial activism refers to the role of the judiciary in interpreting the Constitution and shaping the law. According to NCERT, judicial activism has been instrumental in promoting social justice and protecting the rights of marginalized groups in India.

concepts.title: Panchayati Raj Institutions
concepts.description: Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) are local self-government bodies that play a crucial role in rural development. The 73rd Amendment to the Constitution in 1992 gave constitutional status to PRIs, making them more effective in implementing development programs.

concepts.title: Remembering Constitutional Amendments
concepts.description: To remember the important constitutional amendments, use the mnemonic '42, 44, 73, 74, 86'. These correspond to the 42nd Amendment (1976), 44th Amendment (1978), 73rd Amendment (1992), 74th Amendment (1992), and 86th Amendment (2002), which are significant for Indian politics.

concepts.title: Confusing Between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles
concepts.description: Students often confuse fundamental rights with directive principles. Fundamental rights are enshrined in Part III of the Constitution and are justiciable, while directive principles are in Part IV and are non-justiciable. Remember, fundamental rights are like 'commands' that the government must follow, while directive principles are like 'guidelines' for policy-making.

concepts.title: Key Features of the 73rd and 74th Amendments
concepts.description: The 73rd Amendment (1992) dealt with the establishment of Panchayati Raj Institutions, while the 74th Amendment (1992) dealt with the establishment of Nagar Palikas (municipalities). Remember, the 73rd Amendment is for rural areas, and the 74th Amendment is for urban areas.

concepts.title: National Front and United Front Governments
concepts.description: The National Front government (1989-1990) and the United Front government (1996-1998) were significant coalition governments in India. They marked a shift from single-party dominance to coalition politics, which has become a characteristic feature of Indian politics since the 1990s.

concepts.title: Women's Representation in Politics
concepts.description: The issue of women's representation in politics is a significant topic in Indian politics. The 73rd and 74th Amendments reserved one-third of the seats in local self-government bodies for women, which has increased their participation in politics.
